meta.note: Faithful, extractive summaries; every item links to its original source.

items.headline: Judge approves Anthropic’s $1.5 billion copyright settlement with authors
items.summary: Anthropic blocks authors from opting out of $1.5B settlement at last minute.

items.headline: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
items.summary: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

items.headline: Introducing the ChatGPT for small business program
items.summary: OpenAI launches the ChatGPT for Small Businesses program, helping entrepreneurs build AI skills, automate work, and grow with ChatGPT Work.

items.headline: Google announces Gemini 3.6 Flash and cybersecurity AI, teases 3.5 Pro and Gemini 4
items.summary: There are new 3.6 and 3.5 models today, but Google is already training Gemini 4.

items.headline: Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book piracy settlement approved by judge
items.summary: A federal judge has signed off on Anthropic's $1.5 billion class action settlement with authors who accused the company of training its AI models on copyrighted books, as reported earlier by Reuters. In an order on Monday, Judge Araceli…

items.headline: How much energy do data centers and artificial intelligence use?

items.headline: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
items.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

items.headline: Introducing Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
items.summary: We’re introducing new Gemini models, including Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite and 3.5 Flash Cyber.

items.headline: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
items.summary: Google is launching Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly…

items.headline: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
items.summary: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.

items.headline: Halliday’s latest smart glasses feature a much-improved display
items.summary: I first slipped on Halliday's original smart glasses at CES 2025. I was not a fan. The glasses had a tiny, movable display window embedded into the frame that was incredibly finicky to see, and my 30-minute demo left me with achy eyeballs.…

items.headline: The Download: Chinese AI divides the White House, and a record copyright payout

items.headline: America needs to stop getting shocked by Chinese AI
items.summary: Last week, two Chinese AI companies unveiled models they say can credibly compete with the best systems from OpenAI and Anthropic. The response was swift and predictable. Markets wobbled, commentators declared Silicon Valley shooketh, and…

items.headline: Advancing next-gen AI with materials science innovation

items.headline: Gritt exits stealth with $32 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
items.summary: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plans to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.
